---
description-meta: |
  Three dissertation chapters with direct paths to chapter summary pages and repositories.
title: "Research Atlas"
page-layout: full
---

<div class="surface">
<p class="eyebrow">Three dissertation chapters</p>
<h2>Shortcuts first, chapter pages second</h2>
<p>This page is the front door. Each chapter card stays short: title, main focus, and repository link. Click into the chapter page to see the concise research summary with visual result, contribution, data, logic, and methods.</p>
<div class="hero-actions">
<a class="action-link" href="working_papers.html">Open Spatial Decision Lab</a>
<a class="action-link ghost" href="https://github.com/jaeseokh" target="_blank">GitHub Profile</a>
<a class="action-link ghost" href="../resume/Resume_Jaeseok.pdf" target="_blank">Resume PDF</a>
</div>
</div>

<div class="chapter-grid">
<div class="chapter-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Chapter 1</p>
<h3>Transferability of Multi-Field Information</h3>
<p class="hub-focus">Main focus: whether a model trained in one field can be trusted in a structurally different field.</p>
<div class="hub-link-row">
<a href="chapter1.html">Open chapter page</a>
<a href="https://github.com/jaeseokh/value_multifield_python" target="_blank">GitHub repo</a>
</div>
</div>

<div class="chapter-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Chapter 2</p>
<h3>Information Validity and Profitability Risk</h3>
<p class="hub-focus">Main focus: which part of weak profitability comes from the decision and which part comes from external shocks.</p>
<div class="hub-link-row">
<a href="chapter2.html">Open chapter page</a>
<a href="https://github.com/jaeseokh/corn_seed_response" target="_blank">GitHub repo</a>
</div>
</div>

<div class="chapter-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Chapter 3</p>
<h3>Value of Information in Stochastic Environments</h3>
<p class="hub-focus">Main focus: when monitored signals change decisions enough to create real economic value.</p>
<div class="hub-link-row">
<a href="chapter3.html">Open chapter page</a>
<a href="https://github.com/jaeseokh/value_multifield_ofpe" target="_blank">GitHub repo</a>
</div>
</div>
</div>

## Shared Spine {.unnumbered}

<div class="metric-grid">
<div class="metric-card">
<div class="metric-label">Spatial data engineering</div>
<div class="metric-value">GeoPandas workflows, field boundaries, API and SQL ingestion, reproducible ETL</div>
</div>
<div class="metric-card">
<div class="metric-label">Validation design</div>
<div class="metric-value">Out-of-location holdouts, model comparison, uncertainty checks, sensitivity analysis</div>
</div>
<div class="metric-card">
<div class="metric-label">Model families</div>
<div class="metric-value">XGBoost, Random Forest, Bayesian hierarchical models, GAMs, causal forests</div>
</div>
<div class="metric-card">
<div class="metric-label">Decision lens</div>
<div class="metric-value">Profitability, downside risk, information value, structural reliability</div>
</div>
</div>

## Why This Page Is Short {.unnumbered}

<div class="tone-strip">
<div class="process-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Shortcut</p>
<h3>One page to choose the chapter</h3>
<p>This page is intentionally light. It lets a recruiter, advisor, or collaborator choose the chapter first and then open the more focused summary page.</p>
</div>

<div class="process-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Next Public Layer</p>
<h3>Spatial Decision Lab</h3>
<p>After the dissertation atlas, the next public-facing page is the <a href="working_papers.html">Spatial Decision Lab</a>, which will hold the new API and index-driven spatial research platform.</p>
</div>
</div>
